rulings.Case Title: IN RE: J.G. WENTWORTH ORIGINATIONS LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 3, PETITIONER J.G. WENTWORTH ORIGINATIONS LLC'S Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Payment Rights. Hearing required. Given the high discount rate, the Court questions whether the proposed transfer should be approved, including whether it is fair, reasonable, and in the payee's best interest, considering the totality of the circumstances. (Ins. Code Sec. 10139.5(b).) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Excluding Expert Testimony Of Expert Witness Disclosed By Defendant Xelan Prop 1, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK's Motion For Order Excluding Expert Testimony Of Expert Witness Disclosed By Defendant Xelan Prop 1, LLC Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No ruling. Summary Judgment was granted in favor of Plaintiff UMPQUA BANK on 8/24/20 rendering the present motion moot. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@lewisbrisbois.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER LEE VS. SAFEWAY SERVICES GP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Safeway Services Gp, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint [Stipulated Continuance From April 8, 2020; Briefing Schedule To Track New Hearing Date; Email Dated 7/23/2020]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 6, Defendant Safeway Services GP, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint. Defendant Safeway Service G.P., Inc's demurrer to complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the "cause of action" for gross negligence. The law does not recognize an independent cause of action for gross negligence under the instant circumstances. (See Saenz v. Whitewater Voyages, Inc. (1990) 226 Cal.App.3d 758, 766, fn. 9 ["In reality, California does not recognize a distinct cause of action for 'gross negligence' independent of a statutory basis."].) Moreover, the gross negligence allegations are unnecessary because plaintiff is not attempting to avoid a waiver/release of liability. (Cf. Eriksson v. Nunick (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 826, 856 [defendant moving for summary judgment based on a release needed to present evidence that it did not act with gross negligence because complaint contained allegations of gross negligence]; Chavez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 632 [discussing gross negligence in the context of a release]; Rosencrans v. Dover Images, Ltd (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th1072 [same].) The demurrer to the "cause of action" for punitive damages is moot because that issue is addressed in the motion to strike. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 2, 2-DEFENDANT KATHRYN DEVINCENZI's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Inspection And Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: RICKY SU VS. SUSAN C. NGOON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission, Set One, And Amended Requests For Admission, Set One, Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Susan C. Ngoon, An Individual, Susan C. Ngoon, Trustee Of The Ngoon Family Trust, And Their Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 4, PLAINTIFF RICKY SU's Motion To Have Requests For Admission, Set One, And Amended Requests For Admission, Set One, Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Susan C. Ngoon, An Individual, Susan C. Ngoon, Trustee Of The Ngoon Family Trust, And Their Counsel Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion and Motion to have their Requests For Admission, Set One, and Amended Requests for Admission, Set One, Deemed Admitted and for Monetary Sanctions against Defendants Susan C. Ngoon, an Individual, Susan C. Ngoon, Trustee Of The Ngoon Family Trust, and their counsel is DENIED. The Court has read and considered the Declaration of Priscilla Lao and understands that verifications to the Requests for Admission were furnished to counsel Mr. Purtell and Mr. Obrien on August 5, 2020, well before the hearing on this matter. The mutual requests for sanctions are DENIED. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@lewisbrisbois.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: HUAN ZHENG FANG ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants City And County Of San Francisco And Ahjae P. Holdman Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Against Defendant Ahjae P. Holdman And Paragraph 18 Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 27, 2020, Line 16, Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Ahjae P. Holdman's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Against Defendant Ahjae P. Holdman And Paragraph 18 Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Ahjae P. Holdman's motion to strike punitive damages is granted. In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code Sec. 3294(a).) "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code Sec. 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.) The mere carelessness or ignorance of the defendant does not justify the imposition of punitive damages. (Pacific Gas and Electric Company v. Superior Court (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1170-76; see also Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872 ["conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement of 'oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied,' within the meaning of section 3294."].) Defendants' motion to strike Paragraph 18 of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is denied. The allegations that Defendant Holdman knew or should have known of the dangerous nature of the intersection are relevant to Plaintiffs' negligence claim. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
